prompt.role: Python development environment engineer working on macOS/Linux
prompt.task: Bootstrap the isolated Python working environment for the FlashcardQuizzer project and install every dependency declared in requirements.txt.

context.operating_system: macOS / Linux with zsh or bash

prompt.commands: - python3 -m venv venv - source venv/bin/activate - pip install --upgrade pip - pip install -r requirements.txt
prompt.success_criteria: - A ./venv directory exists at the project root and is activatable - pip is upgraded to the latest available release inside the venv - Every package listed in requirements.txt installs without errors

prompt.role: Senior Python engineer establishing a Definition of Done for the FlashcardQuizzer project
prompt.task: Wire up the project's Definition of Done so that completion is gated by three automated checks: test execution, code formatting, and static type checking.

context.existing_layout: utils/, tests/, and main.py at the project root
context.preferred_config_location: A single pyproject.toml at the project root

prompt.tools: - pytest: execute the unit test suite under ./tests and report coverage of ./utils - black: enforce a single canonical Python code style across the repository - mypy: perform static type checking on the production code in ./utils and main.py
prompt.success_criteria: - pytest runs the full suite and rejects coverage below 80% - black --check exits cleanly with zero files needing reformatting - mypy reports zero errors against utils/ and main.py - A single command runs the three checks sequentially and fails on the first failing step

prompt.role: Senior Python CLI engineer building the FlashcardQuizzer entry point
prompt.task: Refactor main.py into a CLI scaffold so that "python main.py --help" lists every available flag and "python main.py --mode sequential --file data/glossary.json" loads the glossary without crashing.

context.application: FlashcardQuizzer — a terminal flashcard quiz tool
context.starting_point: main.py is currently a TaskManager demo and must be replaced by the CLI entry point
context.data_file: data/glossary.json — a JSON array of objects with string "front" and "back" fields

prompt.requirements: - Expose --help (and -h) listing every flag with its purpose and default value - Expose --file as a required path that must point to an existing readable file - Expose --mode as a choice of sequential, random, adaptive — default sequential — where adaptive is described as prioritizing cards the user gets wrong - Validate the glossary JSON: top-level array of objects, each with string "front" and "back" fields, and reject an empty list - Report how many cards were loaded and which mode was selected, then exit cleanly - Leave the actual interactive quiz loop for a later iteration and print a clear "not yet implemented" notice

constraints.dependencies: Use the click package already declared in requirements.txt; no new dependencies
constraints.scope: Do not delete utils/task_manager.py or utils/file_handler.py — they remain available for later iterations

prompt.success_criteria: - python main.py --help prints all three flags with descriptions and defaults - python main.py --mode sequential --file data/glossary.json runs without crashing and reports the card count - An invalid or missing --file value errors out with a useful message - black --check main.py and mypy main.py both pass

prompt.role: Senior Python engineer designing the data ingestion layer for the FlashcardQuizzer application
prompt.task: Build a data-loading subsystem that reads a JSON glossary file, accepts two structural shapes, validates every card, and surfaces any failure as a user-friendly message instead of a Python traceback.

context.entry_point: main.py wraps the loader inside a click command and is responsible for printing the friendly message and exiting non-zero
context.module_location: utils/data_loader.py — new module, separated from the CLI layer per separation of concerns
context.accepted_shapes: - Array format: a top-level JSON array of card objects - Object format: a top-level JSON object wrapping a "cards" array
context.card_shape: Each card is a JSON object with non-empty string "front" and "back" fields

requirements.functional_requirements: - Read and parse the JSON file at the given path - Accept either the array format or the object-wrapper format transparently - Reject empty card lists with a clear message - Validate every card has non-empty string "front" and "back" fields - Return a list of immutable Flashcard records preserving file order
requirements.error_handling_requirements: - Missing file, permission errors, and other I/O failures must produce a friendly message - Malformed JSON must include the line and column from the parser - Per-card validation errors must include the 1-based card index in the message - main.py must catch the loader's exception, print "Error: <message>" to stderr, and exit with code 1 — never a Python traceback

constraints.architecture_principles: Separation of concerns: I/O parsing and validation live in utils/data_loader.py; the CLI in main.py only orchestrates and reports
constraints.dependencies: Standard library only for the loader (json, dataclasses, pathlib); no new third-party packages
constraints.error_surface: Expose a single FlashcardDataError exception so callers have one type to catch

prompt.class_under_test: utils.data_loader.load_flashcards and the FlashcardDataError it raises
prompt.test_categories: - Happy paths: array format, object format, multi-card ordering, immutability of returned cards - I/O errors: missing file, malformed JSON, unreadable file (POSIX only) - Top-level shape errors: wrong type at root, object missing the cards field, cards not an array, empty list in either format - Per-card shape errors: card not an object, missing front/back, non-string front/back, blank-string front/back, error message reports correct 1-based index for non-first card
prompt.success_criteria: - python main.py --file data/glossary.json (array format) loads cards and exits 0 - python main.py --file data/glossary_object.json (object format) loads cards and exits 0 - Malformed or invalid input produces a single-line friendly message on stderr and exit code 1, with no traceback - pytest suite passes with overall coverage at or above 80% - black --check and mypy both pass on the new and modified files
